heading: Mental health services delivered via telehealth
label: 254c–16 Mental health services delivered via telehealth

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 1 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a public or nonprofit private telehealth provider network that offers services that include mental health services provided by qualified mental health providers.

children.heading: Qualified mental health professionals
children.label: 2 Qualified mental health professionals
children.text: The term “qualified mental health professionals” refers to providers of mental health services reimbursed under the medicare program carried out under title XVIII of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) who have additional training in the treatment of mental illness in children and adolescents or who have additional training in the treatment of mental illness in the elderly.42 U.S.C. 1395

children.label: A
children.text: Children and adolescents in mental health underserved rural areas or in mental health underserved urban areas.

children.label: B
children.text: Elderly individuals located in long-term care facilities in mental health underserved rural or urban areas.

children.heading: Telehealth
children.label: 4 Telehealth
children.text: The term “telehealth” means the use of electronic information and telecommunications technologies to support long distance clinical health care, patient and professional health-related education, public health, and health administration.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the Office for the Advancement of Telehealth of the Health Resources and Services Administration, shall award grants to eligible entities to establish demonstration projects for the provision of mental health services to special populations as delivered remotely by qualified mental health professionals using telehealth and for the provision of education regarding mental illness as delivered remotely by qualified mental health professionals using telehealth.

children.heading: Populations served
children.label: 2 Populations served
children.text: The Secretary shall award the grants under paragraph (1) in a manner that distributes the grants so as to serve equitably the populations described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(3).

children.label: i
children.text: to provide mental health services, including diagnosis and treatment of mental illness, as delivered remotely by qualified mental health professionals using telehealth; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to collaborate with local public health entities to provide the mental health services; and

children.label: i
children.text: to provide mental health services, including diagnosis and treatment of mental illness, in long-term care facilities as delivered remotely by qualified mental health professionals using telehealth; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to collaborate with local public health entities to provide the mental health services.

children.label: B
children.text: pay qualified mental health professionals on a reasonable cost basis as determined by the Secretary for services rendered.

children.label: A
children.text: purchase or install transmission equipment (other than such equipment used by qualified mental health professionals to deliver mental health services using telehealth under the project involved); or

children.label: B
children.text: build upon or acquire real property.

children.heading: Equitable distribution
children.label: d Equitable distribution
children.text: In awarding grants under this section, the Secretary shall ensure, to the greatest extent possible, that such grants are equitably distributed among geographical regions of the United States.

children.heading: Application
children.label: e Application
children.text: An entity that desires a grant under this section shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary determines to be reasonable.

children.heading: Report
children.label: f Report
children.text: Not later than 4 years after , the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report that shall evaluate activities funded with grants under this section.October 26, 2002

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section, $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2002 and such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 2003 through 2006.
